title: Clinic Nurse
summary: The Clinic Nurse position is an essential clinical role, providing onsite clinical support to multiple Pulmonary and Sleep clinic's day to day operations. The Clinic Nurse must hold an RN degree, BSN preferred and have experience within the nursing field. Working in conjunction with the RN team and clinic staff, they will assist in patient call triaging, medication management, RN visits, care coordination as well as other operational and clinical duties. It is necessary that within this role, the Clinic Nurse cultivates and supports a strong team culture and collaborative environment centered on consistently delivering exceptional patient care. This position is based in our South Clinic located in Parker, CO.
responsibilities: Promote the CCPSA culture of team collaboration, while providing excellent clinical care to our patients.
Triage patient calls and elevate to APP team or MD as needed.
Schedule audio visits as needed for certain triage patients needing priority assistance and APP advise
Schedule established patient follow up appointments as needed
Assist in the coordination and management of our Lung Health Initiative program
Build strong positive communication with clinic staff to increase the success of the practice and provide excellent patient care.
See patients during nurse visits to assess patient needs.
In collaboration with the Providers, develop patient education on medications and disease states.
Build strong positive working relationships and communication with Providers.
Manage medications for patients; including education, prior authorizations when applicable, and necessary monitoring.
Conduct care coordination for our patients.
Conduct care coordination and evaluation process for Endobronchial Valve candidates in conjunction with our IP physicians and care team
requirements: Minimum of 1-3 years' experience in the nursing / healthcare field.
Strong clinical knowledge, ability to assess and triage patients over the phone and in person.
Demonstrate knowledge of creating excellent patient experiences in an outpatient clinic setting.
Superior communication skills to effectively and professionally communicate via email, telephone and in person with patients, Providers and staff.
Respect patient privacy by maintaining confidentiality in accordance to HIPAA requirements.
Excellent organizational and time management skills.
Computer proficiency, knowledge of EMR systems.
physicalRequirements: Ability to safely and successfully perform the essential job functions consistent with the ADA, FMLA and other federal, state and local standards, including meeting qualitative and/or quantitative productivity standards.
Ability to drive between clinic locations for meetings.
Ability to sit for extended time periods.
Must be able to lift and carry up to 30 lbs.
Must be able to talk, listen and speak clearly on telephone.
compensation: Comprehensive medical benefits. Company covers 80-85% of individual or family premium.
Dental and vision benefits offered at no cost to the employee. Company covers 100% of individual or family premium.
Optional additional life & disability insurance at group discounts.
401k benefits with company contribution after eligibility period.
